---
title: "Untitled"
output: html_document
date: "2025-10-20"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Load libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)

library(flexclust)
library(parallel)
library(ggplot2)
library(envalysis)
library(paletteer)
library(ggtext)

library(corrplot)
library(scales)


cl <- makeCluster(2, type = "PSOCK")
clusterCall(cl, function() require("flexclust"))


library(umap)
library(uwot)

#for pca analysis
library(factoextra)

#for permanova analysis
library(vegan)
library(pairwiseAdonis)

```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
Filter_Unnorm_Ves <- read.csv("D:/Downloads/Drug Stimulated INS1E Cells/CSV_files_revisions/Unnorm/Filtered_All_Cond_Unorm_vesicles_revised.csv")
Filter_Unnorm_Ves

```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
Filter_Norm_Ves <- Filter_Unnorm_Ves %>% 
  filter(`Geometric.Diameter..nm.` != 0) %>% #Remove weird 0 sized diameters 
  mutate(across(c(LAC.Value, LAC.Std.Dev, Geometric.Diameter..nm., Min.Distance.to.PM.EDT, Mito.Dist.EDT), ~ scale(.)[, 1])) %>%  #Scale approperiate columns
  select(-c(Cell, Condition)) #Remove categories for analysis 
Filter_Norm_Ves
```

```{r}
#Stability Analysis 
Fin_All_Cond_bfc <- bootFlexclust(Filter_Norm_Ves, k = 2:9, nboot = 100, FUN = kcca, multicore = cl)
plot(Fin_All_Cond_bfc)
```

```{r}
fin_All_Cond_clust<- kcca(Filter_Norm_Ves, k = 5, family = kccaFamily("kmeans"))
image(fin_All_Cond_clust)
barchart(fin_All_Cond_clust, shae = TRUE)
```

```{r}
Filter_Norm_Ves_log <- Filter_Unnorm_Ves %>% 
  mutate(across(c(Mito.Dist.EDT, Min.Distance.to.PM.EDT), ~ log10(. + 0.01))) %>%  #log transform distance metrics 
  filter(`Geometric.Diameter..nm.` != 0) %>%  #Remove weird 0 sized diameters 
  mutate(across(c(LAC.Value, LAC.Std.Dev, Geometric.Diameter..nm., Min.Distance.to.PM.EDT, Mito.Dist.EDT), ~ scale(.)[, 1])) %>%  #Scale approperiate columns
  select(-c(Cell, Condition)) #Remove categories for analysis 
Filter_Norm_Ves_log

```

```{r}
#Stability Analysis 
Fin_All_Cond_log_bfc <- bootFlexclust(Filter_Norm_Ves_log, k = 2:9, nboot = 100, FUN = kcca, multicore = cl)
plot(Fin_All_Cond_log_bfc)
```

```{r}
#transforming result for suitable visualization 

rand_indices_fin <- Fin_All_Cond_log_bfc@rand
rand_df_fin <- data.frame(Cluster = rand_indices_fin)
rand_df_fin_long <- rand_df_fin %>% pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "Cluster", values_to = "RandIndex")
rand_df_fin_long

#ggplot

Clust_Stab_fin <- ggplot(rand_df_fin_long, aes(x = Cluster, y = RandIndex)) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = Cluster)) +
  
  scale_x_discrete(labels = as.character(2:9)) +
  guides(fill = "none") +
  labs(x = "Number of Clusters", y = "Rand Index"
       #title = "Global Stability Analysis", subtitle = "K Medians", 
       ) +
  theme_publish() +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = rel(1.8), hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = rel(1.8)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = rel(1.5)),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = rel(1.5)),
  )
Clust_Stab_fin

#ggsave("Clust_Stab_fin_rev.png", plot = Clust_Stab_fin, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 500, bg = "white")
```

```{r}
fin_All_Cond_clust_log<- kcca(Filter_Norm_Ves_log, k = 5, family = kccaFamily("kmeans"))
image(fin_All_Cond_clust_log)
barchart(fin_All_Cond_clust_log, shae = TRUE)
```

```{r}
#Stability Analysis w/ k-medians
Fin_All_Cond_log_med_bfc <- bootFlexclust(Filter_Norm_Ves_log, k = 2:9, nboot = 100, FUN = kcca, multicore = cl, family = kccaFamily("kmedians"))
plot(Fin_All_Cond_log_med_bfc)
```

```{r}
fin_All_Cond_clust_log_med<- kcca(Filter_Norm_Ves_log, k = 5, family = kccaFamily("kmedians"))
image(fin_All_Cond_clust_log_med)
barchart(fin_All_Cond_clust_log_med, shae = TRUE)
```

```{r}
#Add in clusters to dataset

#Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust <- Filter_Norm_Ves_log %>% 
#  mutate(clusters = clusters(fin_All_Cond_clust_log_med))
#Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust
#write.csv(Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust, "FinClust_02.csv", row.names = FALSE)

#Test with PC's
#Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust <- Filter_Norm_Ves_log %>% 
#  mutate(clusters = clusters(pca_df_4_clust))
#Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust
##############################

Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust <- read.csv("FinClust_02.csv")

#Relabel things to adjust be 1 > 5 by order of LAC (lowest to highest LAC)
recluster_id <- c("2" = "5",
                  "5" = "4",
                  "4" = "3",
                  "1" = "2",
                  "3" = "1"
                  )
#ID changing 
Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust_id <- Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust %>%
  mutate(clusters = recode(clusters, !!!recluster_id))
Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust_id

###############################

#long format
Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust_long <- Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust %>%
  group_by(clusters) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), median)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -(c(clusters)),
    names_to = "Parameter",
    values_to = "Z_Score"
  ) %>%
  mutate(clusters = as.character(clusters)) #%>%
  #mutate(clusters = recode(clusters, !!!recluster_id))



Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust_long
```

```{r}
#Presets

#Parameter labels 
cust_param_labels <-  c(
    "Mito.Dist.EDT" = "Distance from Mitochondria",
    "Min.Distance.to.PM.EDT" = "Distance from PM",
    "LAC.Value" = "LAC Mean",
    "LAC.Std.Dev" = "LAC Std. Dev.",
    "Geometric.Diameter..nm." = "Granule Diameter"
  )

#what columns to show/order of columns
show_col <- rev(c("LAC.Value", "Geometric.Diameter..nm.", "LAC.Std.Dev", "Min.Distance.to.PM.EDT", "Mito.Dist.EDT"))

facet_title_labels <- c(
  "1" = "Subpopulation 1 \n (24%)",
  "2" = "Subpopulation 2 \n (20%)",
  "3" = "Subpopulation 3 \n (20%)",
  "4" = "Subpopulation 4 \n (22%)",
  "5" = "Subpopulation 5 \n (14%)"
)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#ggplot
#only show certain columns(filter), then use have them in correct order(factor)
Profile_bar <- ggplot(Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust_long %>% filter(Parameter %in% show_col), 
       aes(x = factor(Parameter, show_col), 
           y = Z_Score, 
           fill = clusters)) +
  
  #Reorient Z-Score. subtract -1.5, then shift y axis by the same amout
  geom_col(aes(y = Z_Score - (-1.5) )) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = function(x) x + (-1.5),
                     breaks = c(0,0.5,1,1.5,2,2.5,3,3.5),
                     limits = c(0, NA),        # Start from 0, auto upper limit
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +

  #Flip axis and facet. Use  correct x axis labels
  coord_flip(clip = "off") +
  facet_wrap(~clusters, nrow = 2, labeller = as_labeller(facet_title_labels), axes = "all_x") +  #Use custom facet labels
  scale_x_discrete(labels = cust_param_labels, limits = show_col) +  #show only certain rows and use custom labels

  #graph design
  guides(fill = FALSE) +
  labs(y = "Z-Score", x = "") + 
  theme_publish() +
  #theme(
  #    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "bisque"),
  #    strip.text.x = element_markdown(size = rel(1.65), hjust = 0.5),
  #    axis.title.x = element_text(size = rel(2), hjust = 0.5),
  #    axis.text.x = element_text(size = rel(1.5)),
  #    axis.text.y = element_text(size = rel(1.5)),
  #    #plot.margin = margin(5.5, 11.0, 5.5, 5.5)
  #) 
  theme(
      #strip.background = element_rect(fill = "bisque"),
      strip.text.x = element_text(size = rel(2.3), hjust = 0.5),
      panel.spacing = unit(2.5, "lines"),
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = rel(2.2), hjust = 0.5),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = rel(1.8)),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = rel(2.2)),
      plot.margin  = margin(t = 5.5, r = 30, b = 5.5, l = 5.5)
      #text = element_text(family = "Arial")
      ) 

Profile_bar
```
```{r}
#ggsave("Fin_ProfilePlot_2row.png", plot = Profile_bar, 
ggsave("PCtest_Fin_ProfilePlot_2row_tmp.png", Profile_bar, 
       width = 16, height = 9, 
       dpi = 1500, bg = "white", 
       )

```


```{r}
#UMAP Creation
#dataset
Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust_id

#umap fitting
#umap_resultParams <- umap(Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust_id[, -8], n_neighbors = 100, min_dist = 0.2, metric = "euclidean", n_components = 2)
umap_resultParams <- umap(Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust_id[, -8], n_neighbors = 10, min_dist = 0.9, metric = "euclidean", n_components = 2)
#umap_resultParams <- umap(Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust_id[, -8], n_neighbors = 5, min_dist = 0.5, metric = "euclidean", n_components = 2)


#Create dataframe for UMAP
umap_df <- as.data.frame(umap_resultParams)
umap_df
colnames(umap_df) <- c("UMAP1", "UMAP2")
umap_df$Cluster <- as.character(Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust$clusters) # Add cluster labels back
umap_df

#Add in condition labels 
Cond_Ves <- Filter_Unnorm_Ves %>% 
  filter(`Geometric.Diameter..nm.` != 0) #Remove weird 0 sized diameters 
umap_df$Condition <- as.character(Cond_Ves$Condition)

umap_df
```

```{r}
Facted_UMAP_Parameter_df <- cbind(umap_df, Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust_id)
Facted_UMAP_Parameter_df

UMAP_Plot <- ggplot(Facted_UMAP_Parameter_df, aes(x = UMAP1, y = UMAP2, 
                                                  color = clusters,
                                                  )) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1, 
             #color = "#f1b032"
             ) +
  
  labs(color = "Subpopulation") + 
  theme_classic() +
  guides(color = guide_legend(
    override.aes = list(size = 9),
    keywidth = unit(1.5 , "cm"),
    default.unit= "cm",
    nrow = 1,
    title.position = "top",           # title above the keys
    )) + 
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = rel(3.8), hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = rel(3.8)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = rel(3.5)),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = rel(3.5)),
    
    
    legend.text = element_text(size = rel(3)),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 43),
    legend.key.size = unit(1.5, "cm"),
    
     legend.position = "bottom",
    #legend.position = c(0.15, 0.15),
    legend.justification = c(0, 0),
    legend.spacing.x = unit(1.5, "cm")

  
) 

UMAP_Plot

#ggsave("UMAP_Fin_weight_rev_bw.png", plot = UMAP_Plot,  dpi = 500, width = 13, height = 11)

```

```{r}
# PCA Creation
# dataset
Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust_id

# Perform PCA
pca_result <- prcomp(Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust_id[, -8], center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

# Create dataframe for PCA results (first 2 principal components)
pca_df <- as.data.frame(pca_result$x[, 1:2])
colnames(pca_df) <- c("PC1", "PC2")

# Add cluster labels back
pca_df$Cluster <- as.character(Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust$clusters)

# Add condition labels (removing weird 0-sized diameters)
Cond_Ves <- Filter_Unnorm_Ves %>%
  filter(`Geometric.Diameter..nm.` != 0)

pca_df$Condition <- as.character(Cond_Ves$Condition)

# Inspect the PCA dataframe
head(pca_df)

# (Optional) Visualize PCA
ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Cluster, shape = Condition)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8, size = 3) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = "PCA of Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust_id",
    x = "Principal Component 1",
    y = "Principal Component 2"
  )

```
```{r}
summary(pca_result)


fviz_eig(pca_result, addlabels = TRUE, ylim = c(0, 100))

#scree plot?
#library(car)
#vif(lm(as.matrix(Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust_id[, -8]) ~ 1))

```
```{r}
eig <- pca_result$sdev^2
pct <- eig / sum(eig) * 100
df_scree <- data.frame(PC = seq_along(pct), Variance = pct)

Scree_plot <- ggplot(df_scree, aes(PC, Variance)) +
  geom_point() + geom_line() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = df_scree$PC) +
  labs(
    #title = "Scree Plot", 
    x = "Principal Component", y = "Variance Explained (%)") +
  theme_publish() +
  theme(
    #plot.title = element_text(size = rel(2.2)),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = rel(2.2), hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = rel(2)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = rel(2)),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = rel(2.5)),
  ) 
Scree_plot
ggsave("Scree_plot_PCA.png", plot = Scree_plot, width = 8, dpi = 500, height = 6, bg = "white")
```
```{r}
# Variable loadings (correlations with PCs)
pca_result$rotation
```

```{r}

# Visualize variable contributions to PCs
Loadings_pca <- fviz_pca_var(pca_result, col.var = "contrib") +
  #scale_color_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red", midpoint = 15) +
  
  #scale_color_viridis_c(option = "cividis_r") + 
  labs(title = "", color = "Relative\nContribution", fill = "Contribution", x = "PC1 (30%)", y = "PC2 (18%)") +  
  theme_publish() +
  theme(
    #legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(size = rel(1.2)),   # larger title text
    legend.text  = element_text(size = rel(1.2)),     # larger tick labels
    legend.key.height = unit(0.5, "cm"),            # taller colorbar
    legend.key.width  = unit(0.8, "cm"),             # wider colorbar
    #plot.title = element_text(size = rel(2.2)),
    
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = rel(1.8), hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = rel(1.6)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = rel(1.5)),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = rel(1.3)),
  ) +
  guides(
    title.position = "top"
  )
Loadings_pca
ggsave("Loadings_PCA.png", plot = Loadings_pca, width = 8, dpi = 500, height = 6, bg = "white")
```
```{r}
cor(Filter_Unnorm_Ves$LAC.Value, Filter_Unnorm_Ves$Mito.Dist.EDT)
```

```{r}
PCA_Plot <- ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Cluster)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 0.8, 
             #color = "#f1b032"
             ) +
  
  labs(
    x = "PC1 (30%)",
    y = "PC2 (18%)",
    color = "Subpopulation"
    ) + 
  theme_classic() +
  guides(color = guide_legend(
    override.aes = list(size = 9),
    keywidth = unit(1.5 , "cm"),
    default.unit= "cm",
    nrow = 1,
    title.position = "top",           # title above the keys
    )) + 
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = rel(3.8), hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = rel(3.8)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = rel(3.5)),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = rel(3.5)),
    
    
    legend.text = element_text(size = rel(3)),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 43),
    legend.key.size = unit(1.5, "cm"),
    
    legend.position = "bottom",
    #legend.position = c(0.15, 0.15),
    legend.justification = c(0, 0),
    legend.spacing.x = unit(1.5, "cm")
    ) +
   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-10, 5))

PCA_Plot

#ggsave("PCA_Fin_weight_rev.png", plot = PCA_Plot,  dpi = 500, width = 13, height = 11)
#ggsave("FINAL_test_PCA_Fin_weight_rev.png", plot = PCA_Plot,  dpi = 500, width = 13, height = 11)

```
```{r}
# Create dataframe for PCA results (first 2 principal components)
pca_df_4 <- as.data.frame(pca_result$x[, 1:5])
colnames(pca_df) <- c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3", "PC4")
pca_df_4

#Stability Analysis 
#pca_df_4_bfc <- bootFlexclust(pca_df_4, k = 2:9, nboot = 100, FUN = kcca, multicore = cl)
#plot(pca_df_4_bfc)

pca_df_4_clust_tmp<- kcca(pca_df_4, k = 5, family = kccaFamily("kmedians"))
image(pca_df_4_clust_tmp)
barchart(pca_df_4_clust_tmp, shae = TRUE)
#km <- kmeans(cluster_data, centers = 3)
```

```{r}
# Create dataframe for PCA results (first 2 principal components)
pca_df_4 <- as.data.frame(pca_result$x[, 1:5])
colnames(pca_df) <- c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3", "PC4")
pca_df_4

#Stability Analysis 
#pca_df_4_bfc <- bootFlexclust(pca_df_4, k = 2:9, nboot = 100, FUN = kcca, multicore = cl)
#plot(pca_df_4_bfc)

pca_df_4_clust_tmp<- kcca(pca_df_4, k = 5, family = kccaFamily("kmedians"))
image(pca_df_4_clust_tmp)
barchart(pca_df_4_clust_tmp, shae = TRUE)
#km <- kmeans(cluster_data, centers = 3)

```
```{r}
write.csv(clusters(pca_df_4_clust_tmp), "FinClust_03.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```


```{r}
#operations to construct the proper dataframe to make the stacked cluster. 1) list of conditions after the diameter = 0 filter, 2) Add in list of conditions, 3) Count number of vesicles per conditions, 4) construct stacked dataframe and compute percentages of condition per clusters, 5) adjust for # of vesicles per condition

#Get conditions when filtered
Cond_array <- Filter_Unnorm_Ves %>% 
  filter(`Geometric.Diameter..nm.` != 0) %>%
  select(Condition) #Remove categories for analysis 

#Dataframe normalized, with clusters and conditions 
Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust_Cond <- Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust %>%
  mutate(Condition = Cond_array$Condition) %>%
  mutate(clusters = as.character(clusters)) %>%
  mutate(clusters = recode(clusters, !!!recluster_id))  #reorder accoring to LAC

Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust_Cond

#Counts of each condition
Cond_counts <- Cond_array %>%
  count(Condition)
Cond_counts

#Formatting for stacked bar chart
Fin_Stacked_Condition_Cluster_graph <-  Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust_Cond   %>% 
  select(-c(LAC.Value, LAC.Std.Dev, LAC.Skew, LAC.Kurtosis, Geometric.Diameter..nm., Min.Distance.to.PM.EDT, Mito.Dist.EDT))  %>% 
  #percentage calculation
  count(Condition, clusters) %>%
  group_by(clusters) %>%
  mutate(percentage = n / sum(n) * 100) 
Fin_Stacked_Condition_Cluster_graph
```
```{r}
#Order_Fin_Stacked_Condition_Cluster_graph


#Subpopulation Shifts
Order_Fin_Stacked_Condition_Cluster_graph <- Fin_Stacked_Condition_Cluster_graph
#Custom "ordering" modifications
Order_Fin_Stacked_Condition_Cluster_graph$Condition <- factor(
  Order_Fin_Stacked_Condition_Cluster_graph$Condition,
  levels = (c("No Stim", "Glucose", "GLM", "GKA", "TAK", "GIP", "EXT"))
  )
facet_condition_labels <- c(
  "No Stim" = "No Stim",
  "Glucose" = "Glucose",
  "GLM" = "GLM",
  "GKA" = "GKA",
  "TAK" = "TAK",
  "GIP" = "GIP",
  "EXT" = "Ex-4"  # <-- This changes just this one
)

#ggplot
Subpopulation_Shifts <- ggplot(Order_Fin_Stacked_Condition_Cluster_graph, aes(x = clusters, y = percentage, fill = clusters)) +
  geom_bar(stat= "identity") +
  facet_wrap(~Condition, nrow = 1, labeller = as_labeller(facet_condition_labels))  +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +

  #graph design
  labs(x = "Clusters", y = "Percentage")+
  theme_publish() +
  guides(fill = FALSE) +
  theme(
    #strip.background = element_rect(fill = "bisque"),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size = rel(1.65), hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = rel(1.5), hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = rel(1.5)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = rel(1.5)),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = rel(1.5)),
    #plot.margin = margin(5.5, 11.0, 5.5, 5.5)
  ) 

##################################################

Order_Fin_adjust_Stacked_Condition_Cluster <- Fin_adjust_Stacked_Condition_Cluster
#Custom "ordering" modifications
Order_Fin_adjust_Stacked_Condition_Cluster$Condition <- factor(
  Order_Fin_adjust_Stacked_Condition_Cluster$Condition,
  levels = (c("No Stim", "Glucose", "GLM", "GKA", "TAK", "GIP", "EXT"))
  )

Subpopulation_Shifts_adjust <- Subpopulation_Shifts %+% Order_Fin_adjust_Stacked_Condition_Cluster 

##################################################
#Clusters in each condition add up to 100%

Adjust_Cond100_subpop <- Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust_Cond %>%
  select(c(clusters, Condition)) %>%
  group_by(Condition) %>%  #keep everything grouped by condition
  count(clusters) %>%
  mutate(percentage = n / sum(n) * 100)
Adjust_Cond100_subpop

#Custom "ordering" modifications
Adjust_Cond100_subpop$Condition <- factor(
  Adjust_Cond100_subpop$Condition,
  levels = (c("No Stim", "Glucose", "GLM", "GKA", "TAK", "GIP", "EXT"))
  )


Subpopulation_Shifts_adjust_100 <- Subpopulation_Shifts %+% Adjust_Cond100_subpop + coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 55))

##################################################

nrow1_Subpopshift_adjust100 <- Subpopulation_Shifts_adjust_100 %+% facet_wrap(~Condition, nrow = 1, labeller = as_labeller(facet_condition_labels)) + labs(x = "Subpopulations") + theme(panel.spacing = unit(1.5, "lines"))
nrow1_Subpopshift_adjust100

#ggsave("nrow1_Subpopulation_Shifts_adjust_100_rev.png", plot = nrow1_Subpopshift_adjust100, width = 15, height = 4, dpi = 1500, bg = "white")
```
```{r}
nrow1_Subpopshift_adjust100_black <- nrow1_Subpopshift_adjust100 +
    #facet_wrap(~Condition, nrow = 1, strip.position = "top", labeller = as_labeller(facet_condition_labels))  +

  theme(
    text = element_text(color = "white"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "white"),
    axis.title = element_text(color = "white"),
    plot.title = element_text(color = "white"),
    legend.text = element_text(color = "white"),
    legend.title = element_text(color = "white"),
     axis.line = element_line(color = "white", linewidth = 0.7),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "white"),
      strip.placement = "outside"

  )
nrow1_Subpopshift_adjust100_black

#ggsave("nrow1_Subpopshift_adjust100_black.png", plot = nrow1_Subpopshift_adjust100_black, width = 15, height = 4, dpi = 1500, bg = "black")

```

```{r}
#hist(Filter_Prim_Unnorm_Ves$LAC.Value, breaks = 30)

INS1E_LAC_hist <- ggplot(Filter_Unnorm_Ves, aes(x = LAC.Value)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "blue") +
  
  labs(x = expression("LAC Mean ("*μ*m^{-1}*")")
       , y = "Frequency", title = "LAC Mean of INS-1E ISGs") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = pretty_breaks(n = 6)) +  # ~6 ticks on x-axis
  
  theme_publish() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = rel(2.2)),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = rel(2.2), hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = rel(2)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = rel(2)),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = rel(2.5)),
  ) 
INS1E_LAC_hist

tmp_Filter_Unnorm_Ves <- Filter_Unnorm_Ves %>%
  filter(!(Geometric.Diameter..nm. > 500 | Geometric.Diameter..nm. == 0))
tmp_Filter_Unnorm_Ves
INS1E_Diam_hist <- INS1E_LAC_hist %+% 
  tmp_Filter_Unnorm_Ves +
  aes(x = Geometric.Diameter..nm.) + labs(x = "Granule Diameter (nm)", title = "Granule Size of INS-1E ISGs")  
INS1E_Diam_hist

INS1E_PMdist_hist <- INS1E_LAC_hist %+% aes(x = Min.Distance.to.PM.EDT) + labs(x = "Normalized EDT Distance to PM", title = "Distance of INS-1E ISGs from PM") + theme( plot.margin = unit(c(1.5, 1, 1, 1), "cm"))
INS1E_PMdist_hist

#ggsave("INS1E_PMDist_rev.png", plot = INS1E_PMdist_hist, width = 9, height = 7, dpi = 500, bg = "white")

```
```{r}
Unnorm_Ves_stats <- read.csv("D:/Downloads/Drug Stimulated INS1E Cells/CSV_files_revisions/Unnorm/All_Cond_Unorm_vesicles_revised.csv")
Unnorm_Ves_stats_tmp <- Unnorm_Ves_stats %>%
  filter(!(Geometric.Diameter..nm. > 500 | Geometric.Diameter..nm. == 0)) %>%
  select(-c("Centroid.X", "Centroid.Y", "Centroid.Z", "Group", "Cell", "Condition")) %>%
  rename(LAC.Mean = LAC.Value)
Unnorm_Ves_stats_tmp

SXT_corr_mat <- cor(Unnorm_Ves_stats_tmp)
SXT_corr_num <- corrplot(SXT_corr_mat, method = 'number')
SXT_corr_circle <- corrplot(SXT_corr_mat)

#png("SXT_corr_circle_highres.png", width = 2000, height = 2000, res = 300)
#corrplot(SXT_corr_mat)  # this is the "corr_circle" plot
#dev.off()
```

```{r}
#Prepare correct dataset for use for figure

Norm_wCluster_df <- Filter_Unnorm_Ves %>% 
  filter(`Geometric.Diameter..nm.` != 0) %>%   #Remove weird 0 sized diameters 
  mutate(cluster = Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust_id$clusters) %>%
  mutate(across(c(LAC.Value, LAC.Std.Dev, Geometric.Diameter..nm., Min.Distance.to.PM.EDT, Mito.Dist.EDT), ~ scale(.)[, 1]))  #Scale appropriate columns
Norm_wCluster_df  

Norm_wCluster_grouped <- Norm_wCluster_df %>%
  mutate(Cell = factor(Cell, levels = paste("Cell", sort(as.numeric(gsub("Cell ", "", unique(Cell))))))) %>% #Sort cells as numerical factors, and not alphabetically 
  group_by(Cell, cluster, Condition) %>%
  summarise(across(where(is.numeric), mean, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -(c(Cell,cluster, Condition)),
    names_to = "Parameter",
    values_to = "Weighted_Z_Score"
  )
Norm_wCluster_grouped

```
```{r}
#Create how well each subpopulation represents each cell

facet_5labs <- c("1" = "Subpopulation 1",
                "2" = "Subpopulation 2",
                "3" = "Subpopulation 3",
                "4" = "Subpopulation 4",
                "5" = "Subpopulation 5"
)

Heatmap_AllCell <- ggplot(Norm_wCluster_grouped, aes(x = Parameter, y = Cell, fill = Weighted_Z_Score)) +
  #main logic of graph
  geom_tile() +
  facet_wrap(~cluster, nrow = 2, axes = "all_x", labeller = labeller(cluster = facet_5labs)) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits=rev) +

  #color scheme
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "E",
                       limits = c(-1.0, 2.5), #limits based on 1% and 99% quantiles )
                       oob = scales::squish, #Deals with values that are not NA values(oob function), and then clamp values to nearest limit (scales::squish)
                       name = "Weighted Z-Score") +
  #legend 
  guides(
    fill = guide_colorbar(
    title.position = "top",
    title.hjust = 0.5,
    barwidth = unit(8, "cm"),
    barheight = unit(1, "cm")
    )
  ) +
  
  #labels and sizing 
  labs(x = "", y = "")+
  theme_publish() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = rel(2.5)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1, size = rel(3)),
    strip.text = element_text(size = rel(3.5)),
    
    legend.margin = margin(b = 0),
    legend.title = element_text(size = rel(2.5)),
    legend.text = element_text(size = rel(2.5))
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c('Diameter','LAC Kurt','LAC Skew','LAC Std Dev.', 'LAC Mean', 'PM Dist', 'Mito Dist')) 

Heatmap_AllCell

#ggsave("Heatmap_AllCell_rev.png", plot = Heatmap_AllCell, width = 20, height = 35, dpi = 1000, bg = "white")
```

```{r}
#Prepare dataset for PERMANOVA  
Ecology_Cells <- Filter_Unnorm_Ves %>%
  #Remove weird 0 sized diameters 
  filter(`Geometric.Diameter..nm.` != 0) %>%   
  #add in cluster selection, and turn into characters 
  mutate(cluster = Filter_Norm_Ves_log_clust_id$clusters) %>% 
  #remove parameters number
  select(-c("LAC.Value", "LAC.Std.Dev", "LAC.Skew", "LAC.Kurtosis", "Geometric.Diameter..nm.", "Min.Distance.to.PM.EDT", "Mito.Dist.EDT")) %>%  
  
  group_by(Cell, Condition, cluster) %>%
  #group by all parameters (cell, condition, cluster) and then summarise
  summarise(TotalValue = n(), .groups = "drop") 
    
Ecology_Cells

Ecology_Cells_wide <- Ecology_Cells %>%
  #Correct formatting for NMDS  
  pivot_wider(
      names_from = cluster, 
      values_from = TotalValue
  ) 
Ecology_Cells_wide

Ecology_Cells_wide_nmds <- Ecology_Cells_wide %>%
  #further formatting
  select(-c("Cell", "Condition")) %>%
  as.data.frame()
Ecology_Cells_wide_nmds

Ecology_Cells_wide_nmds_ratio <- (Ecology_Cells_wide_nmds / rowSums(Ecology_Cells_wide_nmds)) * 100 
Ecology_Cells_wide_nmds_ratio
```
```{r}
NMDS_res <- metaMDS(Ecology_Cells_wide_nmds_ratio, distance = "bray")


```
```{r}
nmds_scores <- as.data.frame(scores(NMDS_res)$sites) 
nmds_scores_cond <- nmds_scores %>%
  mutate(
  Condition = Ecology_Cells_wide$Condition
  )
nmds_scores_cond
```
```{r}
ggplot(nmds_scores_cond, aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, color = Condition)) +
  geom_point()
```
```{r}



#Compute centroids per Condition
centroids <- nmds_scores_cond %>%
  group_by(Condition) %>%
  summarise(
    Centroid_X = mean(NMDS1, na.rm = TRUE),
    Centroid_Y = mean(NMDS2, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#Add centroid coordinates to each point (for segment drawing)
plot_data <- nmds_scores_cond %>%
  left_join(centroids, by = "Condition")

```

```{r}

#Create custom levels and change EXT to Ex4
plot_data_ggplot <- plot_data
plot_data_ggplot$Condition <- factor(
  dplyr::recode(as.character(plot_data_ggplot$Condition), "EXT" = "Ex4"),
  levels = c("No Stim", "Glucose", "GLM", "GKA", "TAK", "GIP", "Ex4")
)
plot_data_ggplot
#Change eXT to Ex4 
centroids$Condition <- recode(
  centroids$Condition,
  "EXT" = "Ex4"
)
centroids
```


```{r}
palette_colors <- c("No Stim"="#7b7b7b", "Glucose"="#ED1C24", "GLM"="#FFDE17",
                    "GKA"="#8DC63F", "TAK"="#7F3F98", "GIP"="#56b4e9", "Ex4"="#1C75BC")

#Plot
NMDS_ConditionComposition <- ggplot(plot_data_ggplot, aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, color = Condition)) +
  # points
  geom_point(size = 7, alpha = 0.75) +
  
  # per-condition ellipses (filled lightly)
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = Condition, fill = Condition),
               geom = "polygon", alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
  
  # centroid markers
  geom_point(data = centroids,
             aes(x = Centroid_X, y = Centroid_Y, fill = Condition),
             inherit.aes = FALSE,
             shape = 23, size = 8, 
             #fill = "white", color = "black", 
             #fill = Condition,
             stroke = 0.6) +
  
  # segments from each point to its centroid
  geom_segment(aes(xend = Centroid_X, yend = Centroid_Y),
               alpha = 0.25, linewidth = 1.0, show.legend = FALSE) +
  # nice palette that covers 7 groups
  
  scale_color_manual(values = palette_colors) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = palette_colors) +
  
  coord_equal() +
  
  
  labs(x = "NMDS 1", y = "NMDS 2", color = "Condition\nCentroid", fill = "Condition\nCentroid") +
  theme_publish() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title.align = 0.5,
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = rel(4)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = rel(4)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = rel(3.5)),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = rel(3.5)),
    
    legend.title = element_text(size = rel(3.1)),   
    legend.text = element_text(size = rel(2.8)),  
    legend.key.size = unit(2.0, "cm")
  )
  
NMDS_ConditionComposition
#ggsave("NMDS_Condition_Composition.png", plot = NMDS_ConditionComposition, width = 20, height = 15, dpi = 500, bg = "white")

```


```{r}
#Distance Matrix

perm_dist<-vegdist(Ecology_Cells_wide_nmds_ratio, method='bray')


#Assumptions

dispersion<-betadisper(perm_dist, group=nmds_scores_cond$Condition,type = "centroid")

plot(dispersion)

anova(dispersion)

```
```{r}
TukeyHSD(dispersion)
#earlier why does TukeyHSD work with dispersion?
```

```{r}
#Test

Perma_result<-adonis2( perm_dist~as.factor(nmds_scores_cond$Condition), data=perm_dist,
                       permutations=9999)

Perma_result
```

```{r}
#remove.packages("cli")
#install.packages("cli")
#install.packages('devtools')
#library(devtools)
#install_github("pmartinezarbizu/pairwiseAdonis/pairwiseAdonis")
```
```{r}
pairwise_results <- pairwise.adonis2(
  perm_dist ~ Condition,
  data = nmds_scores_cond,
  permutations = 9999,
  p.adjust.m = "BH"  # optional, if supported in your version
)
pairwise_results
```





Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
